title: Invite and Present
developmental_frame: In the capstone, Communicate becomes hosting. At 9–10 a child can invite people by making a case (Q3), tell the story of what they made (Q1), and talk with visitors by listening (Q2) — and now they pull all three together on a real day, in front of a real audience. Presenting to guests is a stretch but a thrilling one, and the whole year has built toward being able to do it.
authentic_value_meaning: Value here is being a real host: getting people to come, and helping them understand and enjoy what you show. It's the year's communication skills made real — inviting, storytelling, and talking with guests all in one afternoon.

motivation_lever.mechanism: The child hosts real guests at their Showcase Day, presenting what they made all year. A real audience — coming because they were invited well, then listening to the child's story — is deeply motivating. The child is briefly the star of their own event, which pulls out their best, bravest communicating.

artifact: Real invitations made for Showcase Day, plus a short 'welcome and tour' the child gives visitors on the day, telling the story of what they made this year.
success_criteria: The child invited real guests with a genuine reason to come.
They prepared and rehearsed a short spoken welcome/tour.
On the day they presented to guests and answered questions by listening.

lessons.title: Invite people well
lessons.concept: An invitation is a little case: give people a real reason to come. Use your 'make your case' skill — tell them what they'll see and why it'll be fun, not just when and where.
lessons.real_example: 'Come to my Showcase Day! I'll show the inventions and digital story I made this year, and there's a stall with things I made — plus snacks!' That's an invite people actually want to say yes to.
lessons.apply_task: Make invitations for your guests. Include a real reason to come, plus the date and time from your plan.
lessons.reflection_prompt: Which part of your invite is most tempting? A good reason to come is what fills the room.
lessons.feedback_task: Show an invite to a parent and ask if they'd want to come based on it.
lessons.teach_back_task: Help a sibling write an inviting invitation for something of theirs.
lessons.ai_judgment_note: Invite your real people in your own warm words — a heartfelt invite from you beats a fancy one from a computer.
success_criteria: Real invitations with a reason to come were made and given out.

lessons.title: Prepare your story
lessons.concept: Guests will want to know about what you made. Use your storytelling skill: prepare a short beginning-middle-end story of your year — what you tried, what was tricky, what you're proud of.
lessons.real_example: 'At the start of the year I could barely finish a project (beginning). I learned to break things into steps and fix real problems (middle). Now I've made all this — my favourite is my invention (end).' A real story of your year.
lessons.apply_task: Write a short beginning-middle-end story of your year's making to tell your guests.
lessons.reflection_prompt: What are you proudest of from this year? That belongs in the 'end' of your story.
lessons.feedback_task: Tell your year-story to a parent and ask if it's clear and interesting.
lessons.teach_back_task: Ask a family member to tell *their* beginning-middle-end of something they learned this year.
lessons.ai_judgment_note: This is your real year and your real pride — only you can tell it truthfully. Use your own memories, not a made-up story.
success_criteria: A short three-part story of the year is prepared.

lessons.title: Practise your welcome
lessons.concept: Rehearse a short 'welcome and tour' — greet guests, tell your year-story, and point out what you made. Practising a few times means you'll feel calm and proud on the day instead of frozen.
lessons.real_example: Good hosts rehearse. 'Welcome! Let me show you around — here are my inventions, and this is the digital story I made…' Said a few times in practice, it flows easily on the day.
lessons.apply_task: Practise your welcome and tour out loud two or three times, using your best voice and expression (from Q1).
lessons.reflection_prompt: Where do you stumble? Those spots need one more practice — that's what rehearsing is for.
lessons.feedback_task: Do your welcome for a family member as a dress rehearsal. Ask what would make it warmer or clearer.
lessons.teach_back_task: Show someone why hosts rehearse, using your before/after.
lessons.ai_judgment_note: Rehearsing your real voice for a real welcome is all you — a screen can't practise being a warm host for you.
success_criteria: The welcome/tour was rehearsed until it flowed.

lessons.title: Host on the day
lessons.concept: On Showcase Day, welcome your guests, tell your story, show your work — and when they ask questions, use your listening skill to really hear them and answer. You're the host now: everything this year has led here.
lessons.real_example: A guest asks 'how did you make the phone stand?' You listen, then tell them the story of that invention. That back-and-forth — presenting and listening — is you being a real host.
lessons.apply_task: On Showcase Day, give your welcome, tell your year-story, and answer at least three guest questions by listening carefully.
lessons.reflection_prompt: How did it feel to host? Which part were you proudest of? What surprised you?
lessons.feedback_task: Afterward, ask a guest what they enjoyed most about your welcome and tour.
lessons.teach_back_task: Tell someone who missed it the story of hosting your Showcase Day.
lessons.ai_judgment_note: Hosting real guests is a fully human moment — presenting, listening, answering, all live. This is the year's communication skills, all yours.
success_criteria: The child presented a welcome/tour to real guests.
They answered guest questions by listening and responding.
